vague_intent: post updates about our product launches so the team stays in the loop
true_intent: Whenever we publish a new product launch page, I want a short summary posted to our team's Notion 'Launches' database so everyone can see it. The trigger is a new launch being published; the destination is Notion, not chat.
must_clarify: where to post (which destination platform — Slack vs Notion vs Teams vs email)
what triggers a post (new launch published vs manual vs schedule)
what counts as a 'product launch' / source of launches
must_not_assume: Slack as the destination (the obvious-but-wrong default for 'post updates … team')
a schedule trigger when the real trigger is event-based (a launch being published)
notes: ASSUMPTION TRAP. 'post updates … team stays in the loop' strongly baits Slack, but the real destination is Notion. A build that assumes Slack without asking fails must_not_assume. Also tests trigger disambiguation (event vs schedule).
